title: Active document switches silently between calls
lesson: Photoshop's active document can change between MCP calls (opens, previews, exports). Layers created via app.activeDocument can silently land in the WRONG open document with no error. Start every multi-document script with app.activeDocument = app.documents.getByName('target') and pass that reference around; layer edits on a non-frontmost doc throw 'target document is the frontmost document'.

title: Text layers need UnitValue sizes
lesson: Setting textItem.size (and position) with plain numbers can throw a generic 'Internal error'. Use new UnitValue(n, 'px') for size and position array elements. Set layer.kind = LayerKind.TEXT before touching textItem, and build the SolidColor fully before assigning it.
example: t.size = new UnitValue(150, 'px'); t.position = [new UnitValue(960,'px'), new UnitValue(770,'px')];

title: No ellipse selection in the DOM — use polygon circles
lesson: doc.selection has no ellipse method. Approximate circles with a many-point polygon passed to selection.select: 72 points is visually indistinguishable from a true circle. Then selection.stroke(color, width, StrokeLocation.INSIDE, ...) draws schematic rings.
example: var pts=[]; for (var a=0;a<72;a++){var t=a/72*Math.PI*2; pts.push([cx+r*Math.cos(t), cy+r*Math.sin(t)]);} doc.selection.select(pts);

title: Referencing a removed layer throws 'Object is invalid'
lesson: After layer.remove(), any further property access on that reference throws. When sweeping layers by name in a loop, break out of inner matching loops immediately after remove(), and iterate collections downward (length-1 to 0).

title: Smart Blur EDGEONLY = instant blueprint line art
lesson: artLayer.applySmartBlur(radius, threshold, SmartBlurQuality.HIGH, SmartBlurMode.EDGEONLY) converts a photo to white line-art on black — composite with BlendMode.SCREEN over a colored ground for blueprint/technical looks. Hard-edged isolated subjects extract best. For dark/silhouette sources, run adjustLevels contrast boost first and drop threshold to ~12; duplicate the SCREEN layer to intensify faint linework.

title: Glass distortion (Refrakt look) is scriptable
lesson: artLayer.applyGlassEffect(distortion, smoothness, scaling, invert, TextureType.FROSTED) gives the trendy frosted-glass diffusion. For the ribbed/fluted-glass look, slice vertical strips with selection.select + selection.translate offset on a sine curve; carve 'clear pane' gaps with selection.clear to reveal the sharp layer beneath.

title: Glitch / refraction via selection-translate strips
lesson: Select thin rects and doc.selection.translate them: horizontal strips with random offsets = glitch; vertical strips with sine offsets = ribbed glass refraction. Same primitive, rotated 90 degrees, covers two trend looks.

title: Color Halftone filter is NOT in the scripting DOM
lesson: There is no applyColorHalftone. True halftone dots need low-level ActionManager (charIDToTypeID) calls. Posterize + AddNoise approximates the riso/zine feel scriptably; or ask the user to run Filter > Pixelate > Color Halftone by hand.

title: paste() lands at the document center
lesson: doc.paste() places the pasted layer centered. Reposition by reading layer.bounds and translating by (targetCenter - currentCenter). Fit images into layout cells by resizing the SOURCE doc before copy (scale = min(maxW/w, maxH/h)).

title: Text wiggly selector matchName
lesson: The wiggly selector matchName is 'ADBE Text Wiggly Selector' (not 'ADBE Text Selector Wiggly'). Range selector is 'ADBE Text Selector'; both live under animator.property('ADBE Text Selectors'). Animator properties (Position 3D, Rotation, Opacity) are added under 'ADBE Text Animator Properties'.
example: anim.property('ADBE Text Selectors').addProperty('ADBE Text Wiggly Selector')

title: Seamless loops via cycling Evolution
lesson: For Turbulent Displace / Fractal Noise loops: set the Evolution expression to time * (360 / compDuration) and enable Cycle Evolution with Cycle = 1 revolution — the noise field returns exactly to its start state. Keep any sway/bob expressions on sine periods that divide the comp duration. Verify by diffing first vs last frame of the render (ffmpeg blend=difference; YAVG < 1 is seamless).

title: AppleEvent timeout does not mean the render failed
lesson: ae_render_comp blocks on an AppleScript reply; long renders can outlive the reply timeout while AE keeps rendering. Before re-queueing, check the output file: stable byte size and correct duration (ffprobe) means it completed. Re-running would waste minutes.

title: PSD comp import: durations and anchors need fixing
lesson: Importing a layered PSD as a comp: (1) nested comps (from PSD groups) inherit a short default duration — set .duration on every nested comp AND extend each layer's outPoint; (2) every layer is document-sized with its anchor at the doc center — to rotate a limb around a joint, get the layer's real pixel bounds from Photoshop and set anchorPoint AND position to that pivot (same value keeps it in place at scale 100).

title: Drive effects by matchName; params by index or display name
lesson: Effects.addProperty with matchNames: 'ADBE Turbulent Displace', 'ADBE Glo2' (Glow), 'ADBE Echo', 'ADBE Drop Shadow', 'ADBE 4ColorGradient', 'ADBE Fractal Noise'. Parameters address as '<matchName>-000N' (e.g. 'ADBE Echo-0001' = Echo Time) or by display name fx.property('Amount') in an English UI — wrap display-name access in try/catch.

title: Whips need motion blur enabled at both levels
lesson: Whip-pan and punch transitions read as cheap without motion blur. Set layer.motionBlur = true on the moving layers AND comp.motionBlur = true, with comp.shutterAngle 270-360 for heavy smear. Adjacent whips: move outgoing exactly one frame-width/height and the incoming from one screen away with LINEAR keys so the pair travels as a seamless strip.

title: Glow on already-bright footage blows out white
lesson: Default 'ADBE Glo2' on neon/bright sources nukes the image to white. Check a saved frame after adding glow; usually the fix is removing it or drastically lowering intensity — and move light text OFF bright areas rather than fighting with effects.

title: autoReframeSequence motion preset is a STRING
lesson: sequence.autoReframeSequence(numerator, denominator, motionPreset, newName, useNested) throws 'Illegal Parameter type' if motionPreset is numeric — it must be the string 'default' (slower/faster variants exist). Working call: seq.autoReframeSequence(9, 16, 'default', 'Vertical', false). It runs real AI subject tracking and returns the new sequence.

title: QE DOM transitions: string list, byName lookup, addTransition
lesson: app.enableQE() first. qe.project.getVideoTransitionList() returns a plain ARRAY OF STRINGS (names). Get a transition with qe.project.getVideoTransitionByName(name), apply with qeClip.addTransition(trans, applyToStart, '00:00:00:12') — the timecode string sets duration. Iterate qeTrack.getItemAt(i) over numItems and skip items with empty names (gaps). Modern packs include Whip, Cross Zoom, Spin Motion, Flash, Glitch, VHS Damage, Zoom Blur.

title: Build cut lists by pre-trimming project items
lesson: projectItem.setInPoint(seconds, 4) / setOutPoint(seconds, 4) before app.project.createNewSequenceFromClips(name, orderedItems) yields a butt-cut timeline honoring those trims — no post-hoc ripple editing needed. Premiere snaps to frame boundaries (expect ~30s to come out 29.92 at 25fps).

title: Dynamic-linked AE comps import as 'Comp/Project.aep'
lesson: After importAEComps, the project item is named '<CompName>/<Project>.aep' — exact-name lookups fail. Match with name.indexOf(compName) === 0. Trim an overlay clip by assigning clip.end = (new Time() with .seconds set).

title: exportFramePNG missing in some Premiere versions
lesson: Premiere 26.0 lacks the frame-export API used by ppro_save_frame. Verify sequences by exporting (ppro_export_sequence) and extracting frames from the file with ffmpeg instead.

title: Illustrator exports act on the ACTIVE document
lesson: ai_export / ai_save_preview target whatever document is frontmost — other docs (or ones another tool opened) can be active. Before exporting, set app.activeDocument = app.documents.getByName(...) — or find the right doc by dimensions when names are Untitled. documents.add() also switches the active doc.

title: No blend API — interpolate shapes in a loop
lesson: Illustrator scripting exposes no Blend objects. Fake blends/gradient meshes by generating N intermediate shapes with interpolated geometry and color (e.g. 46 concentric ellipses violet-to-cream = smooth 3D gradient orb). Faux-3D extruded type: stack dark offset text copies under the white top copy.

title: Artboard coordinate system
lesson: artboardRect is [left, top, right, bottom] = [0, H, W, 0] — Y increases UPWARD, and pathItems.ellipse/rectangle(top, left, w, h) take top as the higher Y. Text position is the baseline start point. textFonts.getByName('Arial-BoldMT') in try/catch.

title: Verify visually at every stage
lesson: After each meaningful change, render a preview (ae_save_frame / ps_save_preview / ai_save_preview) and LOOK at it — wrong mouth shapes, blown-out glow, text over busy areas, and mis-parented layers are all invisible in return values. Verify final deliverables from the actual output file (ffmpeg frame extraction), not just the tool result.

title: Pexels direct-download pattern for stock sourcing
lesson: https://images.pexels.com/photos/{id}/pexels-photo-{id}.jpeg?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&w=1600 downloads directly (free license, no attribution). Very old photo IDs can 404 (came back 27 bytes) — check file size/type after curl and keep an alternate ID. Choose isolated, high-contrast subjects when the image will be edge-extracted or cut out.

title: ExtendScript is ES3
lesson: No JSON object, let/const, arrow functions, template literals, Array.map/filter/forEach inside the apps. Use var, string concat, and index loops. AE collections are 1-indexed (comp.layer(1) is the top layer); PS artLayers are 0-indexed. The MCP harness JSON-serializes your return value for you.

title: Extra layers ride along in CH puppet groups
lesson: Character Animator auto-rigs from the layer-name taxonomy (+Character root, Head/Body groups, 14 visemes, Blink layers). Extra non-taxonomy layers (logos, badges) placed INSIDE a group (e.g. Body) simply ride with that group — safe way to add art to a rigged puppet. Remove stray empty layers at root before import.
